utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present with plan name and costs (monthly and annual). Fully specified per requirement (physical and mental health insurance with costs) – Included. 2) property_coverage: Present with costs (monthly and annual) and scope (home/contents insurance with equipment). It specifies 'Enhanced Equipment Coverage' with replacement value for photography equipment; however, the required format asks for home/contents insurance with costs. This appears to focus on equipment rather than home/contents, but it does include contents-like coverage and costs; arguably acceptable as property coverage for a freelancer’s gear – Included. 3) travel_coverage: It says 'Not included' and references an existing plan; the format requires travel insurance with costs. This is not included – Not included. 4) additional_services: Present with services listed and costs (monthly and annual) – Included. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly and annual totals – Included. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are included and sufficiently specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final plan lists: (1) LifeFirst Standard Plan for health coverage, which corresponds to primary_health_coverage.physical_health.LifeFirst Standard Plan. (2) Enhanced Equipment Coverage at €60/month with replacement value for photography equipment; among property_protection.home_content options, the closest match at €60/month with replacement value and suitable for photography gear is LifeFirst Standard Coverage. (3) Travel coverage is not included; thus no mapping. (4) Additional services list Professional Liability Insurance and Disability Insurance, but there are no corresponding items with ratings in the ground-truth dataset, so they cannot be mapped. Therefore, only two items from the final plan map to rated options.
